title: cgroupsv2: podman fails with runc error although crun is configured
body:
Running the latest master, I get the following error:
```
ERRO[0000] oci runtime "runc" does not support CGroups V2: use system migrate to mitigate 
Error: time="2019-10-10T16:06:50+02:00" level=error msg="this version of runc doesn't work on cgroups v2"
this version of runc doesn't work on cgroups v2: OCI x runtime error
```

We must have regressed since v1.6.1 which does not throw this error. I have `/usr/share/containers/libpod.conf` which is configured to use `crun` and v1.6.1 picks that up accordingly. The current master isn't and fails with the above error.

I have no `/etc/containers/libpod.conf`.

comments.body: This is definitely Brent's patch. If you inspect the container, what is the runtime in use?

@mheon it uses runc:

Is this a scratch-build, or a rawhide/F31 RPM? I think we're missing the patch that we carry on top of F31 that resets default OCI Runtime to `crun`

> Is this a scratch-build, or a rawhide/F31 RPM? I think we're missing the patch that we carry on top of F31 that resets default OCI Runtime to `crun`

The v1.6.1 is the F31 RPM, but this one is good. The problem is with the current master.
@vrothberg The patch to swap the default runtime isn't in master - we're carrying it in the RPMs because it's specific to F31 and Rawhide

> @vrothberg The patch to swap the default runtime isn't in master - we're carrying it in the RPMs because it's specific to F31 and Rawhide

The other way around, we seemed to have created a regression in master specific to F31 and cgroups as well. I did not look into the code yet why and where it's breaking but the behavioral change is concerning.
I suspect that the default runtime in `libpod.conf` is not set to `crun` - that's what the patch for F31/Rawhide does, AFAIK
FWIW, here's brent's PR for the check:  https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4216
Strace tells me we're looking at `/usr/local/share/containers/libpod.conf` but we are not looking into `/usr/share` anymore
Is this a make prefix thing? I know the Makefile lets you specify a prefix directory
Yes, that's it, thanks, @mheon ! `PREFIX=/usr make podman` did the trick :+1: Closing the issue.
